description: Fix critical documentation inaccuracies where docs describe APIs, CLI commands, and WebSocket protocol messages that no longer match the actual codebase. The permission model was refactored from enabled/disabled to a 3-state off/ask/auto system, but docs were never updated.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix CLI config set documentation with correct key formats and values
userStories.description: The cli.mdx config set section shows completely wrong key formats (tool.<plugin>_<tool> with enabled/disabled values) instead of the actual format (tool-permission.<plugin>.<tool> with off/ask/auto values). Also claims a non-existent 'trailing dot lists tools' feature and is missing the plugin-permission key format entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: Config set table shows key format `tool-permission.<plugin>.<tool>` with values `off | ask | auto`
Config set table shows key format `plugin-permission.<plugin>` with values `off | ask | auto`
No references to `browser-tool.<name>` key format remain (browser tools use `tool-permission.browser.<tool>`)
No references to `enabled` or `disabled` as config values remain in the config set section
The false claim about trailing-dot listing tool names is removed
Example commands use correct key format: `opentabs config set tool-permission.slack.send_message auto`
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx (lines 615-696 need rewriting)

Context:
The permission model was refactored from a binary enabled/disabled system to a 3-state permission system (off/ask/auto). The CLI config set handler was rewritten but the docs were never updated.

The actual key formats are defined in `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts` lines 166-177:
```
const TOOL_PERMISSION_PREFIX = 'tool-permission.';
const PLUGIN_PERMISSION_PREFIX = 'plugin-permission.';
const LOCAL_PLUGINS_ADD = 'localPlugins.add';
const LOCAL_PLUGINS_REMOVE = 'localPlugins.remove';
const PORT_KEY = 'port';
```

The SUPPORTED_KEYS help text:
```
Supported keys:
  tool-permission.<plugin>.<tool>   Set a per-tool permission (value: off | ask | auto)
  plugin-permission.<plugin>        Set a plugin-level default permission (value: off | ask | auto)
  port                              Set the server port (value: 1-65535)
  localPlugins.add                  Add a local plugin path (value: absolute or relative path)
  localPlugins.remove               Remove a local plugin path (value: path to remove)
```

Changes:
1. Replace the Table at lines 621-656 with the correct 5 key formats (tool-permission, plugin-permission, port, localPlugins.add, localPlugins.remove)
2. Delete the false paragraph at line 673 about trailing-dot listing tools
3. Replace example commands at lines 675-696 with correct examples:
   - `opentabs config set tool-permission.slack.send_message auto` (set a tool to auto-approve)
   - `opentabs config set tool-permission.slack.send_message off` (disable a tool)
   - `opentabs config set plugin-permission.slack ask` (set plugin default to require approval)
   - Keep the port, localPlugins.add, localPlugins.remove examples as-is (those are correct)

Constraints:
- Do NOT change anything outside the config set section (lines 615-696)
- The port, localPlugins.add, and localPlugins.remove rows in the table are already correct — keep them
- The --force flag table (lines 658-671) is correct — keep it

userStories.title: Fix WebSocket config methods and confirmation response in mcp-server.mdx
userStories.description: The mcp-server.mdx Extension→Server message table lists 5 stale config methods (setToolEnabled, setAllToolsEnabled, setToolsEnabled, setBrowserToolEnabled, setAllBrowserToolsEnabled) that don't exist. The actual methods are config.setToolPermission, config.setPluginPermission, and config.setSkipPermissions. Also, the confirmation.response description uses wrong field names.
acceptanceCriteria: The 5 stale config method rows are replaced with 3 actual methods: config.setToolPermission, config.setPluginPermission, config.setSkipPermissions
config.setToolPermission description mentions setting per-tool permission (plugin name + tool name + permission value)
config.setPluginPermission description mentions setting plugin-level default permission
config.setSkipPermissions description mentions toggling the global skip-permissions flag
confirmation.response description says `{ id, decision: 'allow' | 'deny', alwaysAllow?: boolean }` instead of allow_once/allow_always/deny

userStories.title: Fix health endpoint response field name and add missing fields in mcp-server.mdx
userStories.description: The health endpoint JSON example shows 'confirmationBypassed' but the actual field name is 'skipPermissions'. The field description at line 206 also uses the stale name. The discoveryErrors field is missing from the JSON example.
acceptanceCriteria: Health endpoint JSON example uses `skipPermissions` instead of `confirmationBypassed`
The description at line 206 references `skipPermissions` and `OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS`
The JSON example includes the `discoveryErrors` array field
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx (lines 138-209)

Context:
The actual health response is built in `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts` lines 321-353. The field is named `skipPermissions: state.skipPermissions` (not `confirmationBypassed`).

Changes:
1. Line 169: Change `"confirmationBypassed": false` to `"skipPermissions": false`
2. Line 175: The `discoveryErrors` array IS in the example — verify it's there (it is at line 175). If the ordering doesn't match the source, reorder to match.
3. Line 206: Change `confirmationBypassed` to `skipPermissions` and update the description to: `skipPermissions` — whether all permission checks are bypassed (via `OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS=1` env var)

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other fields in the health response — the rest are correct
- Keep the same JSON formatting style

userStories.title: Add missing summary property to ToolDefinition in tools.mdx
userStories.description: The ToolDefinition interface definition and properties table in tools.mdx are missing the optional `summary` property that exists in the actual SDK.
acceptanceCriteria: ToolDefinition interface block includes `summary?: string` between displayName and description
Properties table includes a row for `summary` describing it as a short UI summary that falls back to description
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/tools.mdx (lines 29-97)

Context:
The actual ToolDefinition in `platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts` lines 37-59 includes:
```
/** Short human-readable summary for the UI. Falls back to description if omitted. */
summary?: string;
```

Changes:
1. In the interface definition block (lines 30-43), add `summary?: string;` after line 36 (after `description: string;`)
2. In the Properties table (lines 47-97), add a new Table.Row after the `description` row (after line 70) with:
   - Property: `summary`
   - Type: `string`
   - Description: Short human-readable summary for the UI. Falls back to `description` if omitted. Useful when the full description is too verbose for compact UI layouts.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any existing rows — only add the new summary row
- Match the exact Table.Row formatting pattern used by other rows in the file

userStories.title: Add reviewedVersion field to PluginPermissionConfig in configuration.mdx
userStories.description: The configuration reference shows PluginPermissionConfig with only permission and tools fields, but the actual type also has reviewedVersion which is part of the plugin review system.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginPermissionConfig table includes a row for `reviewedVersion` with type `string`, default empty/undefined
Description mentions it tracks which plugin version was reviewed and triggers re-review when plugins update
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx (lines 112-135)

Context:
The actual PluginPermissionConfig in `platform/shared/src/index.ts` lines 93-101:
```
export interface PluginPermissionConfig {
  permission?: ToolPermission;
  tools?: Record<string, ToolPermission>;
  reviewedVersion?: string;
}
```

Changes:
Add a new Table.Row after the `tools` row (after line 133) in the PluginPermissionConfig table:
- Field: `reviewedVersion`
- Type: `string`
- Default: (none)
- Description: Plugin version that has been reviewed. When present and matching the installed plugin version, the plugin is considered reviewed. When a plugin updates to a new version, the permission resets to `'off'` until re-reviewed. Set automatically by the plugin review flow (`plugin_inspect` → `plugin_mark_reviewed`).

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the existing `permission` or `tools` rows
- Match the exact Table.Row and Table.Cell formatting used by other rows

userStories.title: Remove false claim about [official]/[community] labels in plugin search output
userStories.description: The cli.mdx docs claim that plugin search results are labeled [official] or [community], but the actual CLI output has no such labels.
acceptanceCriteria: No references to `[official]` or `[community]` labels in the plugin search section of cli.mdx
The output description accurately reflects the actual format: package name, version, description, and author
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx (find the plugin search section)

Context:
The actual plugin search output in `platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts` lines 422-436 formats each result as:
```
`  ${pc.bold(pkg.name)} ${pc.dim(`v${pkg.version}`)}${desc ? ` — ${desc}` : ''} ${pc.dim(`by ${author}`)}`
```
There are no [official] or [community] labels anywhere in the output.

Changes:
Search for any mention of `[official]` or `[community]` in the plugin search section. If found in an example output block or description, remove it and replace with the actual output format.

Constraints:
- Only change the plugin search section — do not modify any other command documentation
- If the labels appear in a fenced code block showing example output, replace the entire block with accurate example output
